identificationModule.briefTitle: Restrictive Versus Liberal Rate of Extracorporeal Volume Removal Evaluation in Acute Kidney Injury
identificationModule.officialTitle: REstrictive Versus LIberal Rate of Extracorporeal Volume Removal Evaluation in Acute Kidney Injury

statusModule.whyStopped: The trial was terminated due to insufficient funds.

collaborators.name: National Institute of Diabetes and Digestive and Kidney Diseases (NIDDK)

descriptionModule.briefSummary: Critically ill patients with acute kidney injury and fluid overload who are frequently treated by fluid removal during dialysis are at an increased risk of complications and death. Both slower and faster rates of fluid removal may cause injury to the vital organs. This proposed clinical trial will examine the feasibility of restrictive compared with a liberal rate of fluid removal in order to develop effective treatments for fluid overload and to improve the health of critically ill patients.
descriptionModule.detailedDescription:
"Net ultrafiltration (UFnet)," also known as net fluid removal during kidney replacement therapy, has been used in the treatment of fluid overload among critically ill patients with acute kidney injury (AKI) for more than seven decades. However, the optimal rate of fluid removal (i.e., UFnet rate) remains uncertain, complications such as hypotension and cardiac arrhythmias occur frequently, and more than 40% of patients die. Observational studies in critically ill patients receiving continuous kidney replacement therapy (CKRT) show that UFnet rate has a "J" shaped association with mortality with both slower and faster UFnet rates associated with increased risk of death compared with moderate UFnet rates.

The overall objective of this randomized trial is to establish the feasibility of maintaining patients in the restrictive UFnet rate strategy during treatment with CKRT. The investigator's central hypothesis is that a restrictive UFnet rate strategy embracing a "slow and steady" approach to fluid removal is associated with fewer complications, including cardiac arrhythmias, hypotension, and death, compared with a more liberal "sprint and pause" strategy among critically ill patients.

The trial is a prospective, two-center, unblinded, parallel-group, 2-arm, comparative effectiveness, stepped-wedge cluster-randomized trial among 112 critically ill patients with AKI treated with CKRT in 10 ICUs across two hospital systems. The trial will be conducted at 5 ICUs at University of Pittsburgh Medical Center in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania, as well as 5 ICUs at Mayo Clinic, Rochester, Minnesota. ICUs will be randomized 1:1 to either a restrictive or a liberal UFnet rate strategy. During the first six months, all ICUs will continue with a liberal UFnet rate strategy. Every two months thereafter or when 10 patients have been enrolled, whichever occurs first, one ICU will be randomized to deploy the restrictive UFnet rate strategy.

In the liberal group, the UFnet rate will be titrated between 2.0-5.0 mL/kg/h and maintained throughout fluid removal. In the restrictive group, the UFnet rate will be titrated between 0.5-1.5 mL/kg/h and maintained throughout fluid removal. The UFnet rates used in both strategies are used in current clinical practice.This feasibility trial will be used to support the rationale and design of a future multicenter phase III randomized trial to examine the effects of alternative UFnet rate strategies on patient-centered clinical outcomes.

Amendment changes- The "Delivered UFnet rates" outcome measure was amended based on a new calculation of effect size when we lowered the sample size from 144 patients to 112 patients in the trial.

The Participant Recruitment Rate Over 21 Months" outcome measure was based on a new sample size estimation when we lowered the sample size from 144 patients to 112 patients in the trial due to slow rate of enrollment in the trial.

designInfo.interventionModelDescription: Prospective, two-center, unblinded, parallel-group, 2-arm, comparative effectiveness, stepped-wedge cluster-randomized trial.

armGroups.label: Restrictive UFnet Strategy
armGroups.description: Fluid removal will be titrated to keep net ultrafiltration rate between 0.5-1.5 mL/kg/h

armGroups.label: Liberal UFnet Strategy
armGroups.description: Fluid removal will be titrated to keep net ultrafiltration rate between 2.0-5.0 mL/kg/h

interventions.name: Restrictive UFnet Rate Strategy
interventions.description: In the restrictive group, the UFnet rate will be titrated between 0.5-1.5 mL/kg/h and maintained throughout fluid removal.

interventions.name: Liberal UFnet Rate Strategy
interventions.description: In the liberal group, the UFnet rate will be titrated between 2.0-5.0 mL/kg/h and maintained throughout fluid removal.

primaryOutcomes.description: A minimum separation of 0.53-0.57 mL/kg/h in mean delivered UFnet rates between the two intervention arms.
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Until the end of continuous kidney replacement therapy or day 28, whichever occurs first.

primaryOutcomes.measure: No. of Participants With Protocol Deviation.
primaryOutcomes.description: Protocol deviation defined as delivered UFnet rate that lies \>0.5 mL/kg/h outside of the target UFnet rate range for greater than six consecutive hours.
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Until the end of continuous kidney replacement therapy or day 28, whichever occurs first.

primaryOutcomes.description: An enrollment rate of 1 patient per ICU per time period.

secondaryOutcomes.description: Patient daily fluid balance will be measured while on continuous kidney replacement therapy and reported as average across all study days.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Average daily fluid balance from enrollment to ICU discharge or until day 28, whichever occurs first.

secondaryOutcomes.description: Patient cumulative fluid balance will be measured while on continuous kidney replacement therapy.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Daily from enrollment to ICU discharge or until day 28, whichever occurs first.

secondaryOutcomes.description: The number of days the patient received kidney replacement therapy while in the hospital.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Daily from enrollment to hospital discharge or until day 28, whichever occurs first.

secondaryOutcomes.description: The number of days the patient received mechanical ventilation while in hospital.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Daily from study enrollment to ICU discharge or until day 28, whichever occurs first.

secondaryOutcomes.description: The no. of days the patient remained free of organ failure while in the ICU.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Daily from study enrollment to ICU discharge or until day 28, whichever occurs first.

secondaryOutcomes.description: The number of days patients need to stay in the ICU
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Daily from study enrollment to ICU discharge or day 28, whichever occurs first.

secondaryOutcomes.description: The number of days patients need to stay in the hospital
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Daily from study enrollment to hospital discharge or day 28.

secondaryOutcomes.description: The proportion of patients who died while in the hospital.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: From study enrollment to hospital discharge or day 28, whichever occurs first.

secondaryOutcomes.description: The proportion of patients who were on dialysis at hospital discharge.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: From study enrollment until hospital discharge or day 28, whichever occurs first.

otherOutcomes.measure: No. of Intradialytic Hypotension Episodes
otherOutcomes.description: Intradialytic hypotension will be defined as a new mean arterial pressure \<65 mmHg, systolic blood pressure \<90 mmHg or a decline in systolic blood pressure \>40 mmHg, and/or a \>30% increase in dose of existing vasopressors, initiating a new vasopressor, or administration of fluid bolus with a goal to maintain mean arterial pressure \>=65 mmHg, systolic blood pressure \>=90 mmHg.
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: From study enrollment until 24 hours after termination of continuous kidney replacement therapy or day 28, whichever occurs first.

otherOutcomes.measure: No. of Intradialytic Hypertension Episodes
otherOutcomes.description: Intradialytic hypertension will be defined as new onset systolic blood pressure \>=160 mmHg or mean arterial pressure \>=80 mmHg for more than 1 hour in the absence of any vasopressor or inotrope use.
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: From study enrollment until 24 hours after termination of continuous kidney replacement therapy or day 28.

otherOutcomes.measure: No. of Episodes of Intradialytic Cardiac Arrhythmias
otherOutcomes.description: New onset intradialytic cardiac arrhythmias including supraventricular tachycardia, bradycardia, atrial fibrillation, ventricular tachycardia, ventricular fibrillation, asystole/pulseless electrical activity will be diagnosed as per American Heart Association.
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: From study enrollment until 24 hours after termination of continuous kidney replacement therapy or day 28, whichever occurs first.

otherOutcomes.measure: No. of Participants Who Receive UFnet Rates Higher Than the Assigned Intervention Arm for Treatment of Fluid Overload Emergencies.
otherOutcomes.description: No. of participants in whom emergent use of UFnet rates higher than the assigned treatment arm for more than 3 consecutive hours will be noted.
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: From study enrollment until 24 hours after termination of continuous kidney replacement therapy or day 28, whichever occurs first.

otherOutcomes.measure: No. of Participants With Severe Hypophosphatemia
otherOutcomes.description: Hypophosphatemia defined by serum phosphate \<0.5 mg/dL
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: From study enrollment until 24 hours after termination of continuous kidney replacement therapy or day 28, whichever occurs first.

otherOutcomes.measure: No. of Participants With Severe Hypokalemia
otherOutcomes.description: Hypokalemia defined by serum potassium \<3.0 mg/dL
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: From study enrollment until 24 hours after termination of continuous kidney replacement therapy or day 28, whichever occurs first.

otherOutcomes.measure: No. of Participants With Severe Hypocalcemia
otherOutcomes.description: Hypocalcemia defined by serum calcium \<1.90 mg/dL or ionized calcium \<0.90 mmol/L
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: From study enrollment until 24 hours after termination of continuous kidney replacement therapy or day 28, whichever occurs first.

otherOutcomes.measure: No. of Episodes of Stopping of CKRT System Due to Hemofilter Clotting or Clogging.
otherOutcomes.description: Stopping of CKRT system due to filter clotting and/or clogging.
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: From study enrollment until termination of continuous kidney replacement therapy or day 28, whichever occurs first.

otherOutcomes.measure: No. of Participants With Discontinuation of Fluid Removal Due to Hemodynamic Instability.
otherOutcomes.description: No. of participants in whom fluid removal is stopped due to hemodynamic instability such as intradialytic hypotension, cardiac arrhythmias, or cardiac arrest.
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: From study enrollment until termination of continuous kidney replacement therapy or day 28, whichever occurs first.

otherOutcomes.measure: No. of Participants in Whom Surgical Wounds Are Left Open After Surgery Due to Tissue Edema.
otherOutcomes.description: This will be determined as per the primary surgeon. Abdominal wounds left open for a second look or for abdominal re-exploration will not be counted unless there is concurrent tissue edema precluding abdominal closure.
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: From study enrollment until 24 hours after termination of continuous kidney replacement therapy or day 28, whichever occurs first.

otherOutcomes.measure: No. of Participants With New Organ Dysfunction as Assessed by Change in Sequential Organ Failure Assessement (SOFA) Scoring System From Baseline.
otherOutcomes.description: New organ dysfunction will be assessed by changes in SOFA scores from the baseline across the five organ systems of central nervous system, cardiovascular, respiratory, coagulation, and liver.
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: From study enrollment until 24 hours after termination of continuous kidney replacement therapy or day 28, whichever occurs first.

otherOutcomes.measure: No. of Participants With Worsening of Systolic or Diastolic Cardiac Function on Echocardiogram From Baseline.
otherOutcomes.description: Changes in transthoracic echocardiogram will be assessed from baseline (if available)
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: From study enrollment until 24 hours after termination of continuous kidney replacement therapy or day 28, whichever occurs first.

otherOutcomes.measure: No. of Participants With Worsening of Pulmonary Edema on Chest X Ray and/or CT Scan Based on Radiologist Report.
otherOutcomes.description: Changes in chest X ray and CT scan will be assessed from baseline (if available).
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: From study enrollment until 24 hours after termination of continuous kidney replacement therapy or day 28, whichever occurs first.

otherOutcomes.measure: No. of Participants With Worsening of Ileus on Abdominal X Ray and/or CT Scan Based on Radiologist Report.
otherOutcomes.description: Changes in abdominal X ray and CT scan will be assessed from baseline (if available)
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: From study enrollment until 24 hours after termination of continuous kidney replacement therapy or day 28, whichever occurs first.

otherOutcomes.measure: No. of Participants With Bowel Ischemia or Anastomotic Breakdown Based on Intraoperative Findings.
otherOutcomes.description: Intraoperative findings of bowel ischemia or anastomotic breakdown will be noted as determined by the surgeon (if available).
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: From study enrollment until 24 hours after termination of continuous kidney replacement therapy or day 28, whichever occurs first.

otherOutcomes.measure: No. of Participants With Pressure Ulceration Per Nursing Records.
otherOutcomes.description: No. of participants with new diagnosis of pressure ulcerations as documented by the nursing staff in the electronic medical record (if available)
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: From study enrollment until 24 hours after termination of continuous kidney replacement therapy or day 28, whichever occurs first.

otherOutcomes.measure: No. of Participants With New Wound Infection Per Nursing Records.
otherOutcomes.description: No. of participants with new diagnosis of wound infection as documented by the nursing staff in the electronic medical record (if available)
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: From study enrollment until 24 hours after termination of continuous kidney replacement therapy or day 28, whichever occurs first.

otherOutcomes.measure: No. of Participants With New Arterial and/or Deep Vein Thrombosis as Assessed by Doppler Studies.
otherOutcomes.description: New arterial thrombosis or deep vein thrombosis as assessed by doppler study (if available)
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: From study enrollment until 24 hours after termination of continuous kidney replacement therapy or day 28, whichever occurs first.

otherOutcomes.measure: No. of Participants With Severe Anemia Requiring Red Cell Transfusions.
otherOutcomes.description: Participants with severe anemia requiring red cell transfusions (if available).
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: From study enrollment until 24 hours after termination of continuous kidney replacement therapy or day 28, whichever occurs first.

otherOutcomes.measure: No. of Participants With Severe Thrombocytopenia Requiring Platelet Transfusions.
otherOutcomes.description: No. of participants with severe thrombocytopenia requiring platelet transfusions (if available).
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: From study enrollment until 24 hours after termination of continuous kidney replacement therapy or day 28, whichever occurs first.

otherOutcomes.measure: No. of Participants With New Secondary Infections.
otherOutcomes.description: New diagnosis of secondary infections occurring after initiation of study intervention will be collected based on culture data, antibiotic use and suspected sepsis as per clinician judgment (if available).
otherOutcomes.timeFrame: From study enrollment until 24 hours after termination of continuous kidney replacement therapy or day 28, whichever occurs first.

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

1. Age 18 years or older
2. Stage 3 acute kidney injury according to the Kidney Disease: Improving Global Outcomes (KDIGO) criteria
3. Started or intending to start CKRT for volume management
4. Attending intensivist or nephrologist intending to remove net fluid using CKRT for at least 48 hours

Exclusion Criteria:

1. Respiratory distress due to pulmonary edema or fluid overload in unintubated patients
2. Massive volume infusion (i.e., \>200 mL/h for \>6 hours of continuous infusion)
3. No intention to remove net fluid as determined by attending intensivist or nephrologist
4. Attending intensivist or nephrologist believes that the protocol will not be followed
5. Continuous net fluid removal for \>48 hours prior to study enrollment
6. Patients on chronic outpatient hemodialysis
7. Patients with history of, or current admission for kidney transplantation
8. Patients on comfort measures only orders.
9. Moribund not expected to survive \>24 hours
10. Confirmed pregnancy
11. Patients treated with extracorporeal membrane oxygenation, ventricular assist device, or intra-aortic balloon pump
12. Organ donors with neurological determination of death (i.e., brain dead donors)
13. Drug overdose requiring CKRT for drug clearance
14. Enrollment in a concurrent interventional clinical trial with direct impact on fluid balance (e.g., \>500 mL study drug administration)

ipdSharingStatementModule.description: The RELIEVE-AKI study investigators will review the request for data from other investigators. The investigators will execute a data-sharing agreement with the University of Pittsburgh to share de-identified data with other investigators for research purposes only. The data-sharing agreement will ensure: i.) a commitment to using the data only for research purposes and not to identify any individual participant; ii.) a commitment to securing the data using appropriate computer technology; and iii.) a commitment to destroying or returning the data after analyses are completed on a specific date. The investigators will not release any patient identifiers in any of the data files shared with other researchers. The investigators will also require appropriate confidentiality agreements and approvals from the Institutional Review Boards of other investigators before the data are shared.
ipdSharingStatementModule.timeFrame: Beginning 9 months and ending 36 months after the publication of the primary trial manuscript.
ipdSharingStatementModule.accessCriteria: Investigators whose proposed use of the data has been approved by an independent review committee identified for this purpose.

participantFlowModule.preAssignmentDetails: For this study, time of signing the informed consent will be the study enrollment time. There is no patient-level randomization in this trial. The randomization is at the ICU-level and based on study period of every two months.
participantFlowModule.recruitmentDetails: RELIEVE AKI is a multicenter, prospective randomized controlled trial. This study enrolled 99 patients into the study between July 5, 2022 - June 17, 2024 at UPMC and Mayo Clinic hospitals.

groups.title: Sequence 1 (ICU-1)
groups.description: 22 months of Liberal UFnet strategy followed by 1 month of Restrictive UFnet strategy.

groups.title: Sequence 2 (ICU-2)
groups.description: 20 months of Liberal UFnet strategy followed by 3 months of Restrictive UFnet strategy.

groups.title: Sequence 3 (ICU-3)
groups.description: 14 months of Liberal UFnet strategy and 9 months of Restrictive UFnet strategy.

groups.title: Sequence 4 (ICU-4)
groups.description: 8 months of Liberal UFnet strategy and 15 months of Restrictive UFnet strategy.

groups.title: Sequence 5 (ICU-5)
groups.description: 23 months of Liberal UFnet strategy and 0 months of Restrictive UFnet strategy.

groups.title: Sequence 6 (ICU-6)
groups.description: 12 months of Liberal UFnet strategy and 11 months of Restrictive UFnet strategy.

groups.title: Sequence 7 (ICU-7)
groups.description: 18 months of Liberal UFnet strategy and 5 months of Restrictive UFnet strategy.

groups.title: Sequence 8 (ICU-8)
groups.description: 6 months of Liberal UFnet strategy and 17 months of Restrictive UFnet strategy.

groups.title: Sequence 9 (ICU-9)
groups.description: 10 months of Liberal UFnet strategy and 13 months of Restrictive UFnet strategy.

groups.title: Sequence 10 (ICU-10)
groups.description: 16 months of Liberal UFnet strategy and 7 months of Restrictive UFnet strategy.

outcomeMeasures.title: Daily Fluid Balance (Average)
outcomeMeasures.description: Patient daily fluid balance will be measured while on continuous kidney replacement therapy and reported as average across all study days.
outcomeMeasures.populationDescription: The outcome measure is the average of daily fluid balance (i.e., fluid input minus output over 24 hours) while the patient stayed in the ICU and was compared between the two groups.
outcomeMeasures.timeFrame: Average daily fluid balance from enrollment to ICU discharge or until day 28, whichever occurs first.

Fluid removal will be titrated to keep net ultrafiltration rate between 2.0-5.0 mL/kg/h

Liberal UFnet Rate Strategy: In the liberal group, the UFnet rate will be titrated between 2.0-5.0 mL/kg/h and maintained throughout fluid removal.

outcomeMeasures.title: No. of Intradialytic Hypotension Episodes
outcomeMeasures.description: Intradialytic hypotension will be defined as a new mean arterial pressure \<65 mmHg, systolic blood pressure \<90 mmHg or a decline in systolic blood pressure \>40 mmHg, and/or a \>30% increase in dose of existing vasopressors, initiating a new vasopressor, or administration of fluid bolus with a goal to maintain mean arterial pressure \>=65 mmHg, systolic blood pressure \>=90 mmHg.
outcomeMeasures.populationDescription: The adverse events were compared among participants assigned to the two groups.
outcomeMeasures.timeFrame: From study enrollment until 24 hours after termination of continuous kidney replacement therapy or day 28, whichever occurs first.

outcomeMeasures.title: No. of Intradialytic Hypertension Episodes
outcomeMeasures.description: Intradialytic hypertension will be defined as new onset systolic blood pressure \>=160 mmHg or mean arterial pressure \>=80 mmHg for more than 1 hour in the absence of any vasopressor or inotrope use.

adverseEventsModule.timeFrame: Adverse events for this study were collected from study enrollment until 24 hours after termination of continuous kidney replacement therapy, up to a maximum time frame of 28 days. For example, if continuous kidney replacement therapy was terminated on day 14 on a patient, adverse events were collected until day 15. If continuous kidney replacement therapy continued beyond 28 days, the adverse events were collected only up to 28 days.

eventGroups.title: Restrictive UFnet Strategy
eventGroups.description:
Fluid removal will be titrated to keep net ultrafiltration rate between 0.5-1.5 mL/kg/h

Restrictive UFnet Rate Strategy: In the restrictive group, the UFnet rate will be titrated between 0.5-1.5 mL/kg/h and maintained throughout fluid removal.

eventGroups.description:
Fluid removal will be titrated to keep net ultrafiltration rate between 2.0-5.0 mL/kg/h

Liberal UFnet Rate Strategy: In the liberal group, the UFnet rate will be titrated between 2.0-5.0 mL/kg/h and maintained throughout fluid removal.

seriousEvents.term: Death after transition to comfort measures
Multisystem organ failure resulting in death
Worsening acidosis and hypotension despite up-escalation of pressors
Hemorrhagic and hypovolemic shock secondary to massive hematemesis from GE varices.

limitationsAndCaveats.description: First, clinicians may hold firm beliefs about UFnet titration, and their awareness of treatment assignment may have led to bias in UFnet titration, specifically not to increase UFnet rates in the liberal arm. Second, we did not assess circulating intravascular volume in UFnet titration as no technology has been validated for net fluid removal.

pointOfContact.title: Raghavan Murugan, MD, MS, FRCP, Principal Investigator
